reasoning: Huawei has historically opposed Open RAN, which threatens its dominant position in 5G by breaking vendor lock-in. While there was some speculation in mid-2024 that Huawei might adopt Open RAN to comply with German regulations and stay in that market, recent articles from late 2024 and 2025 show that operators like Deutsche Telekom and Telus are replacing Huawei equipment with Open RAN solutions from other vendors (like Nokia and Samsung). Huawei consistently refuses to open its proprietary interfaces. The crowd forecast on April 2, 2026, was 0.02, and a thorough search of news through April 12, 2026, reveals no announcements or hints of a change in Huawei's strategy. With less than 9 months remaining until the January 1, 2027 deadline, it is highly unlikely Huawei will reverse its core business model and announce commercial Open RAN equipment.
